better-sqlite3.interface: new Database(path); db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON'); db.close()

shape.key_api_note: Migration is a CONSUMER package — it opens the shared registry DB and writes rows through the upstream stores. The import-pipeline + roundtrip tests close + reopen the handle to prove rows persisted (reopen-proves-persistence). Gate on the runner exit code.
parseFrontmatter splits the file at the leading --- YAML fence and parses the block. name: -> slug; description: -> description; tools: comma list -> tokens; model: -> alias.

shape.component_model: Decision 5 head/version split: ComponentStore.create({slug,type,content,isShared?}) inserts head row (registry_components, slug TEXT PK) + version-1 row (registry_component_versions, version_id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT) atomically in a transaction. Each imported .md body section becomes one create() call for a new slug or a version() call to append a new version row to an existing head. UNIQUE(slug,version) + the slug→head FK are DB-enforced, so emitting a version for a missing head or a duplicate (slug,version) now THROWS instead of silently inserting.
shape.importAgent: src/import/import-agent.ts :: importAgent(db, mdPath): parseFrontmatter -> splitBody -> AgentStore.create(AGENT row) -> ComponentStore.create (new slug) or ComponentStore.version (existing slug, changed content) -> dedup by normalized-text hash reuses shared components (no-op if content unchanged) -> CompositionStore.attach({agentSlug, componentSlug, position, versionPin: null, isRequired: true}) writes junction rows. Returns an import result including flagged tokens for human review.
shape.version_pin_on_import: CompositionStore.attach is called with versionPin: null on import (resolve latest at resolve-time). Pin an exact version with attach({ pinVersion: humanVersionNumber }) or attach({ versionPin: versionId }) when needed. See packages/ai/agent-registry/src/store/composition-store.ts:133-161.
shape.resolveVersionId: ComponentStore.resolveVersionId(slug, version): number — maps a (slug, humanVersion) pair to its stable registry_component_versions.version_id. Used when a junction version_pin needs to reference a specific version_id after import. See packages/ai/agent-registry/src/store/component-store.ts:260-280.
shape.dependency_edge: agent-registry-migration DEPENDS ON agent-registry-schema. References AgentStore.create + ComponentStore.create/version/resolveVersionId + CompositionStore.attach. Writes rows into agent-registry's stores (REFERENCES.md 'Agent Definition Files').
shape.key_api_note: Consumed by import-pipeline + skills-migration. Uses REAL stores, not mocks (real-deps-not-mocks).

shape.token_to_canonical: the frontmatter parser resolves each `tools:` token (e.g. 'Bash','Read','Write') via ToolBindingStore.resolve / a lookup WHERE platform_tool_name = token AND platform = claude_code -> canonical tool id ('shell_exec','file_read',...). Unknown tokens land in a `flagged` list (never silently dropped — may need a new MCP_SERVER row).
shape.agent_tool_grants: importAgent writes AgentToolStore grants for the resolved canonical tool ids.
shape.dependency_edge: agent-registry-migration DEPENDS ON agent-tool-registry. References ToolBindingStore.resolve + AgentToolStore.grant. Writes AGENT_TOOL rows.

shape.model_hint_resolution: the frontmatter parser resolves the `model:` alias (e.g. 'sonnet') via the model_platform_bindings[claude_code] (ModelStore) -> canonical modelHint (claude_sonnet_4_6); unknowns flagged. The importer writes AGENT.model_hint from this.
shape.dependency_edge: agent-registry-migration reads agent-provider's model bindings to map a claude_code model alias back to a canonical model id (REFERENCES.md frontmatter mapping table: model: alias -> AGENT.model_hint -> MODEL_PLATFORM_BINDING[claude_code]).
shape.key_api_note: Consumed by frontmatter-parser. Read-only against the seeded provider bindings.

shape.policy_migration: ad-hoc policy in agent frontmatter (allowedAgents arrays, permission lists) is superseded by AGENT_POLICY rows — the importer writes AgentPolicyStore attachments for the policies derived from frontmatter (SCOPE.md 'Ad-hoc Policy in Agent Frontmatter'). max_rework becomes a policy override_config parameter rather than a hardcoded field.
shape.dependency_edge: agent-registry-migration DEPENDS ON agent-policy. References AgentPolicyStore.attach. Writes AGENT_POLICY rows.
shape.key_api_note: Consumed by import-pipeline. This plan runs LAST (it includes the final removal phase) and writes rows into the schema, tool-registry, and policy stores.

compiler-consumed.interface: the REAL `agent-registry compile <slug> --platform claude_code` CLI / compileAgent

shape.equivalence_gate: src/verify/equivalence-gate.ts :: verifyEquivalence(db, mdPath) imports a fixture agent, runs the REAL @adhd/agent-compiler `agent-registry compile <slug> --platform claude_code`, normalizes both the compiled output and the original .md, and diffs them. Empty diff = PASS. buildReport(results) -> { entries, allPass } gates the removal phase — nothing is removed until the round-trip is verified for every agent (REFERENCES.md 'Agent Definition Files'; no data loss).
shape.dependency_edge: agent-registry-migration DEPENDS ON agent-compiler (the round-trip gate drives the real compile). This is in addition to the schema/tool-registry/policy write dependencies — confirmed by scaffold-package.md (deps include @adhd/agent-registry + @adhd/agent-compiler).
shape.key_api_note: Consumed by roundtrip-equivalence-gate. Because agent-compiler already depends on schema/tool-registry/provider/policy, migration depending on the compiler transitively pulls the whole convergence — which is why migration is LAST.
